title: Religious Insignia on Dog Tags Act
short_title: Religious Insignia on Dog Tags Act
plain_summary: This bill tells the Department of Defense to change their rules about dog tags within ninety days. The new rules will let licensed companies make and sell dog tags that show both military symbols and religious symbols together. Before this bill, companies were not allowed to mix military and religious symbols on the same dog tag. The rule change will apply to dog tags made starting in September 2013.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Armed Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Religious Insignia on Dog Tags Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Religious Insignia on Commercial Identification Tags
sections.text: The Secretary of Defense must review and change the rules within ninety days after this law passes. The changes will allow companies that have permission to use military trademarks to make and sell dog tags that combine military symbols with religious symbols. Dog tags are the metal identification tags that military members wear around their necks. The new rules must be treated as if they started working on September thirteenth, two thousand thirteen.
